scope.purpose: Preserve exact observed repository generations, commit lineage, substantive and placeholder content classifications, attribution, license status, open coordination surfaces, migration destinations, and rollback requirements without selecting canonical authority.

substantive_content: PR #1 documentation, governance, architecture, contract, lifecycle, recovery, validation, and Pages candidate

license.effect: No license grant is inferred; reuse and migration remain blocked pending an explicit license decision.

repositories.proposed_destination: candidate charter-history and coordination authority, subject to D1 approval
repositories.attribution_requirement: Preserve commit SHAs and source paths for every migrated or summarized record.

substantive_content: PR #2 consolidation, migration, compatibility, gluing, onboarding, validation, and Pages candidate
placeholder_content: Approximately 140 empty documentation paths created by commit 5294dd9ced84646bb409c0f8d13c4ae469e5620c across AI, API, architecture, developer, diagrams, modules, protocols, QSO, reference, research, standards, and tutorial categories

license.effect: No license grant is inferred; reuse and migration remain blocked pending an explicit license decision.

repositories.proposed_destination: candidate compatibility landing, historical taxonomy, package-name proposal, and migration evidence, subject to D1 approval
repositories.attribution_requirement: Preserve commit SHAs, PR references, and source paths; empty paths may be converted only into explicitly proposed backlog records.

classification_rules.substantive: Contains non-empty product, governance, architecture, migration, onboarding, validation, or release content.
classification_rules.placeholder: Exists as a filename or empty page without evidence-bearing content.
classification_rules.historical: Preserved for provenance but not represented as current authority.
classification_rules.proposed: Candidate content requiring explicit review and approval.
classification_rules.blocked: Cannot progress without an identified decision, owner, contract, evidence generation, or license.

sensitive_data_review.limitations: Repository code search is not a complete secret scan and may not cover binary content, deleted history, inaccessible objects, or unindexed branches. A dedicated history-aware scanner remains required before migration or publication.

current_obstructions: Canonical repository and display/package identity are not approved.
Both repositories lack an explicit LICENSE file.
The active PR candidates are independently validated but not jointly accepted or reconciled.
No approved migration manifest binds each source path to a final destination and resulting-state verification.
Support, security-reporting, compatibility-window, redirect, archive, and rollback owners remain unassigned.
The placeholder taxonomy must not be represented as implemented architecture or completed documentation.

rollback.trigger: Any migration loses attribution, produces competing current authorities, publishes unsupported claims, or cannot be reproduced at the recorded exact heads.
rollback.action: Withdraw the migration candidate, restore both repositories to their last reviewed immutable heads, retain this manifest and failed-candidate evidence, and leave D1 blocked.

skill_tree_mapping.use: claim-evidence narrative, accessible diagrams, and cross-modal consistency
information hierarchy, technical exposition, documentation testing, and lifecycle synchronization
duplicate consolidation, canonical identifier design, provenance, and graph integrity
canonical source resolution, lineage, version substitution detection, hashing, and correction propagation
decision memory, classification, preservation, and onboarding transfer
plain-language status and accessible public explanation
threat-aware acceptance criteria and assurance-case maintenance
repository archaeology, migration risk, cutover planning, and rollback continuity
